description: Fix CLI config and startup UX friction found during fresh-user testing. Three issues: (1) config set leaves stale entries when re-enabling a browser tool or setting port back to default, (2) first-time `opentabs start` output dumps all 4 MCP client configs at once overwhelming new users, (3) `config show --show-secret` dumps the same wall of MCP configs. LOW severity issues not included: audit --file returns exit 0 for 'no file found' (minor inconsistency).

userStories.title: Clean up default-value config entries on config set
userStories.description: As a user, I want `opentabs config set browser-tool.X enabled` to remove the entry from browserToolPolicy (since enabled is the default) so that `config show` doesn't display redundant entries and `config set port 9515` removes the port key (since 9515 is the default).
acceptanceCriteria: Running `opentabs config set browser-tool.browser_execute_script disabled` then `opentabs config set browser-tool.browser_execute_script enabled` results in an empty browserToolPolicy object in config.json (the entry is removed, not set to true)
Running `opentabs config set port 8080` then `opentabs config set port 9515` results in no port key in config.json (the default port entry is removed)
Running `opentabs config set tool.slack_send_message disabled` then `opentabs config set tool.slack_send_message enabled` results in the tool entry being removed from the tools object
config show output does not display browserToolPolicy section entries for tools set to enabled (default)
userStories.notes:
File: platform/cli/src/commands/config.ts

Three places need changes:

1. `handleSetBrowserTool()` (~line 313): After `policy[toolName] = enabled;` (line 341), if `enabled` is true, delete the key instead: `if (enabled) delete policy[toolName]; else policy[toolName] = false;`. Also clean up empty object: if `Object.keys(policy).length === 0`, delete `config.browserToolPolicy`.

2. `handleSetPort()` (~line 350): After setting `config.port = newPort;` (line 359), check if `newPort === DEFAULT_PORT` and if so, `delete config.port;` instead. Import DEFAULT_PORT is already available (line 16).

3. `handleSetTool()` (~line 273): Same pattern — if `enabled` is true, `delete tools[toolName]` instead of `tools[toolName] = true`. Clean up empty object.

Existing tests are in platform/cli/src/commands/__tests__/config.test.ts — update or add test cases to verify cleanup behavior.

userStories.title: Show only the user's MCP client config on first start instead of all four
userStories.description: As a new user running `opentabs start` for the first time, I want to see only one MCP client config snippet (defaulting to Claude Code) with a note about how to see configs for other clients, so that the output is focused and actionable instead of an overwhelming wall of JSON.
acceptanceCriteria: First-time `opentabs start` output shows only the Claude Code MCP config snippet by default (not OpenCode, Cursor, or Windsurf)
After the Claude Code snippet, a line says something like: 'For other MCP clients (Cursor, Windsurf, OpenCode): opentabs config show --show-secret'
The generic catch-all line at the bottom still shows the URL and bearer token for manual configuration
`opentabs start --show-config` shows the same single-client format
userStories.notes:
File: platform/cli/src/commands/start.ts

`printMcpClientConfigs()` (line 200) currently iterates over ALL configs from `getMcpClientConfigs()`. Add a `primaryOnly?: boolean` parameter. When true, only print the first config (Claude Code) and add a hint line after it pointing to `opentabs config show --show-secret` for other clients.

Call sites:
- `printFirstTimeInstructions()` (line 152): pass `primaryOnly: true` — this is the first-time flow where brevity matters most
- The `--show-config` branch at line 276 also calls `printMcpClientConfigs()` — pass `primaryOnly: true` here too
- `handleConfigShow()` in config.ts (line 184): keep showing all configs for `--show-secret` since the user explicitly asked for full output

Do NOT remove the `getMcpClientConfigs()` function or the ability to show all configs — just default to showing one in the startup flow.
